LAW AND MOTION, DEPT. 302, DEFENDANT SONY CORPORATION, SONY CORPORATION OF AMERICA, SONY EUROPE LTD. MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS. MATTER ARGUED. ALL THREE DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO DISMISS THE CLAIMS FILED BY PLAINTIFF GEORGE DAMIGOS ARE DENIED, AND ALL THREE DEFENDANTS' MOTION TO STAY THE CLAIMS OF MR. DAMIGOS PENDING THE RESOLUTION OF THE BELGIAN LAWSUIT ARE DENIED ON THE CONDITION (WHICH MR. DAMIGOS MUST ACCEPT IN WRITING BY MARCH 8, 2017) THAT MR. DAMIGOS NOT MAKE ANY CLAIMS OR SEEK ANY MONETARY RELIEF OF ANY KIND IN THE BELGIAN LAWSUIT FOR WRONGFUL, DISCRIMINATORY OR ABUSIVE TERMINATION OF HIS EMPLOYMENT BY ANY OF THE DEFENDANTS. (SEE FULL WORD MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING). JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN; CLERK: FELICIA GREEN; COURT REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR NO. 8189, EMAIL: CKAREN@SFTC.ORG (302/HK)

Motion To Quash Service Of Summons Or Stay Or Dismiss Filed By Sony Corporation, Sony Corporation Of America, Sony Europe Ltd. (Part 2 Of 2 For Purposes Of Entry Of Tentative Ruling)
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Off Calendar

MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
Off Calendar Mar-02-2017 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Menaka N. Fernando, of Outten & Golden LLP, Tel. 415.638.8800, for plaintiffs Dimitrios Dimitris Kourepis;
Jennifer Schwartz, of Outten & Golden LLP, Tel. 415.638.8800, for plaintiffs Dimitrios Dimitris Kourepis;
Jennifer Sta.Ana, of Levy Vinick Burrell Hyams LLP, Tel. 510.318.7709, for plaintiffs Dimitrios Dimitris Kourepis;
Sharon R. Vinick, of Levy Vinick Burrell Hyams LLP, Tel. 510.318.7702, for plaintiffs Dimitrios Dimitris Kourepis;
Aaron M. Wais, of Mitchell Silberberg & Knupp LLP, Tel. 310.312.3136, for defendants Sony Europe, Sony Corporation of America & Sony Corporation.

Ruling: A R G U E D. DEFENDANT SONY CORPORATION, SONY CORPORATION OF AMERICA, SONY EUROPE LTD. MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS is denied, all three defendants' motion to dismiss the claims filed by plaintiff George Damigos is denied, and all three defendants' motion to stay the claims of Mr. Damigos pending the resolution of the Belgian lawsuit is denied on the condition (which Mr. Damigos must accept in writing by March 8, 2017) that Mr. Damigos not make any claims or seek any monetary relief of any kind in the Belgian lawsuit for wrongful, discriminatory or abusive termination of his employment by any of the defendants. (See full Word Minutes for complete ruling). Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Felicia Green; Court Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR No. 8189, email: ckaren@sftc.org; Dept. 302.
